evals.expected_output: A markdown file written to ~/personal/obsidian/Work/digest/YYYY-MM-DD.md containing all required sections: AWS & Cloud, AI Coding Tools, Programming Languages, Neovim & Editor Ecosystem, Terminal & CLI Tools, and Key Takeaways. Each item has a one-line summary and source link. Major items have 2-3 sentences of detail. Key Takeaways has 4-6 items with significance explained.

assertions.text: A file was written to ~/personal/obsidian/Work/digest/ with today's date as the filename (YYYY-MM-DD.md)
The file has YAML frontmatter with an id field matching the date
The digest contains all required top-level sections: AWS & Cloud, AI Coding Tools, Programming Languages, Neovim & Editor Ecosystem, Terminal & CLI Tools, Key Takeaways
Items include source links in markdown link format [text](url)
Key Takeaways section has between 4 and 6 items
The digest title or content references the date range since the last digest (2026-03-23)

evals.prompt: What's new in tech this week? Generate my update.
evals.expected_output: Same as eval 1 — a complete digest written to the Obsidian vault. The skill should trigger even though the user didn't say 'digest'.

assertions.text: A file was written to ~/personal/obsidian/Work/digest/ with today's date as the filename
The AWS & Cloud section has at least 3 items with source links
The AI Coding Tools section covers Claude and at least one other tool

evals.prompt: Check for any updates to Neovim and AWS since the last digest.
evals.expected_output: A full digest written to the Obsidian vault. Even though the user only asked about Neovim and AWS, the skill should produce the complete structured digest covering all domains.

assertions.text: A file was written to ~/personal/obsidian/Work/digest/ with today's date as the filename
The Neovim & Editor Ecosystem section is present and has content
The AWS & Cloud section is present and has content
